title: [RTL/FIRRTL] Store port names more efficiently
body:
We currently store port name attributes in an extremely inefficient format, as generalized argument attributes.  Consider a module with three input ports like this:

`module @foo(%a: i1, %b: i1, %c: i1) {`

Storing these as argument attributes causes a ton of extra dictionaries to be created, and causes them to be indexed by strings, see the logic that builds this up in `buildModule` in RTLOps.cpp.  The argument attributes are stored something like this:

```
"rtl.module" () { name = @foo,  // name attribute
                            arg0 = { rtl.name = "a"}, 
                            arg1 = { rtl.name = "b"},
                            arg2 = { rtl.name = "c" }} 
```

This is four dictionaryattrs (one for each argument and one overall, and means the op level dictionary attr has #ports entries in it, which slows down everything that is looking up the name for a module - a fairly common operation.  This also means that we can't efficiently look up names by index, because we have to create the "arg1" strings and unique them to do a lookup.

Instead, we should store the names as a separate attribute with a single array attribute, something like:

```
"rtl.module" () { name = @foo,  // name attribute
                            portnames = [ "a", "b", "c"] }
```

This would allow directly indexing port names, make the # entries in the module dictionary constant time.  This would dramatically reduce memory usage and make the compiler significantly faster.

This affects both the RTL and the FIRRTL dialect modules.
